_readme: Metric definitions per lens. Add sourceUrl and retrievedDate when updating values in stateData.json. The rendering code reads this at build time.

patient.source: CDC PLACES 2022–2023 · CDC WONDER 2021 · Census ACS 2024

items.name: Adults reporting fair or poor health
items.defn: Percentage of adults age 18+ who self-reported their general health as fair or poor on the BRFSS survey.
items.method: CDC PLACES uses small-area estimation methodology applied to BRFSS responses, age-adjusted to the 2000 US standard population.
items.caveat: Self-reported. Cultural and linguistic differences in how people describe health may affect cross-state comparison.

items.name: Diabetes prevalence
items.defn: Adults age 18+ ever told by a doctor that they have diabetes (excludes gestational diabetes and prediabetes).
items.method: BRFSS self-reported, small-area estimation via CDC PLACES.
items.caveat: Excludes undiagnosed diabetes. Actual prevalence is estimated to be ~15-20% higher than diagnosed rate.

items.name: Coronary heart disease prevalence
items.defn: Adults age 18+ ever told by a doctor they had coronary heart disease, angina, or had a heart attack.
items.method: BRFSS self-reported, small-area estimation via CDC PLACES.

items.name: Obesity prevalence
items.defn: Adults with BMI 30 or higher calculated from self-reported height and weight.
items.method: BRFSS, small-area estimation via CDC PLACES.
items.caveat: Self-reported height/weight typically underestimates BMI by 1-2 points compared to measured.

items.name: Median age
items.defn: Median age of the total resident population.
items.method: American Community Survey 5-year estimates.

items.name: Cancer mortality rate
items.defn: Deaths from malignant neoplasms (all cancers combined) per 100,000 population.
items.method: Age-adjusted to the 2000 US standard population using NCHS death certificate data.

items.name: Life expectancy at birth
items.defn: Average number of years a newborn would be expected to live if current age-specific mortality rates were to continue.
items.method: Period life table calculations from NCHS mortality data.

items.name: Routine checkup in past year
items.defn: Adults age 18+ reporting a routine checkup with a doctor in the past 12 months.
items.method: BRFSS self-reported, CDC PLACES small-area estimation.

clinical.source: HRSA AHRF 2022 · AANP 2023 · ACGME 2023 · HRSA HPSA 2024 · BLS OEWS 2022

items.name: Primary care physicians per 100k
items.defn: Active patient-care MDs and DOs in family medicine, general internal medicine, general pediatrics, and OB/GYN, per 100,000 population.
items.method: HRSA Area Health Resources File, derived from AMA Physician Masterfile.
items.caveat: Counts physician location of practice, not patient population served. Border-area distortions are common.

items.name: Specialists per 100k
items.defn: Active patient-care physicians in non-primary-care specialties per 100,000 population.
items.method: HRSA AHRF derived from AMA Physician Masterfile.

items.name: Nurse practitioners per 100k
items.defn: Actively licensed nurse practitioners per 100,000 population.
items.method: Aggregated from state nursing boards and HRSA workforce data.
items.caveat: Licensure does not equal active practice. Some NPs hold licenses in multiple states.

items.name: Health Professional Shortage Areas
items.defn: Federal HRSA designations indicating geographic areas, populations, or facilities with shortages of primary care, dental, or mental health providers.
items.method: HRSA designation criteria based on provider-to-population ratios and other factors.

items.name: Population in HPSAs
items.defn: Percentage of state population living within a designated HPSA.
items.method: Population estimates within HPSA boundaries from HRSA.

items.name: Telehealth adoption
items.defn: Share of outpatient visits delivered via telehealth modalities (video, audio-only, etc.).
items.method: Various sources including hospital surveys and payer claims data.
items.caveat: Methodology varies widely. Cross-state comparisons should be interpreted with caution.

items.name: Active medical residents
items.defn: ACGME-accredited residency positions filled per 100,000 population.
items.method: ACGME annual data combined with state population.

items.name: Registered nurses per 100k
items.defn: Registered nurses (RN) employed per 100,000 population, across all care settings including hospitals, clinics, home health, and community nursing.
items.method: Bureau of Labor Statistics Occupational Employment and Wage Statistics (OEWS), SOC 29-1141, divided by Census state population estimate.

items.name: Respiratory therapists per 100k
items.defn: Respiratory therapists employed per 100,000 population, including both Certified Respiratory Therapists (CRT) and Registered Respiratory Therapists (RRT).
items.method: Bureau of Labor Statistics OEWS, SOC 29-1126. Includes all employed RTs regardless of credential level.
items.caveat: BLS employment counts do not distinguish CRT from RRT credential holders. States with high COPD burden typically show higher RT density.

operations.source: AHA Annual Survey 2024 · CMS Care Compare 2023–2024

items.name: Hospital beds per 1,000 population
items.defn: Staffed acute care hospital beds per 1,000 population. Excludes long-term care, psychiatric, and rehabilitation beds.
items.method: AHA Annual Survey of Hospitals.

items.name: Hospital count
items.defn: Total number of registered short-term acute care hospitals.
items.method: AHA registration database, cross-referenced with CMS.

items.name: Critical access hospitals
items.defn: Number of hospitals with CMS Critical Access Hospital designation (max 25 beds, located 35+ miles from nearest hospital).
items.method: CMS designation registry.

items.name: For-profit hospital share
items.defn: Investor-owned hospitals as a percentage of all registered acute care hospitals in the state.
items.method: AHA Annual Survey ownership classification.

items.name: Median ED wait (admit decision)
items.defn: Median minutes from ED arrival to decision to admit, for admitted patients.
items.method: CMS Hospital Inpatient Quality Reporting.
items.caveat: State average masks wide variation between facilities. ED wait time is a small slice of the total ED experience.

items.name: CMS hospital star rating (avg)
items.defn: Average of CMS Overall Hospital Quality Star Ratings across hospitals in the state.
items.method: CMS rating methodology aggregating mortality, safety, readmission, patient experience, and timely effective care measures.
items.caveat: Methodology revised significantly in 2021. Pre-2021 vs post-2021 comparisons not advisable.

items.name: 30-day all-cause readmission
items.defn: Risk-adjusted rate of unplanned readmissions within 30 days of discharge for Medicare beneficiaries.
items.method: CMS hospital-wide readmission measure, risk-adjusted.

payer.source: Census SAHIE 2024 · CMS Enrollment 2023–2024 · CMS Marketplace OEP 2024

items.name: Uninsured rate
items.defn: Percentage of population under age 65 without health insurance coverage at time of survey.
items.method: Census Small Area Health Insurance Estimates (SAHIE), modeled from ACS, Medicaid enrollment, and IRS data.

items.name: Medicaid enrollment
items.defn: Total Medicaid and CHIP enrollees as a share of state population.
items.method: CMS monthly enrollment reports, averaged for the year.
items.caveat: Post-2023 Medicaid unwinding has affected enrollment significantly. Trend interpretation requires context.

items.name: Medicare enrollment
items.defn: Total Medicare beneficiaries (Parts A, B, C, D combined unique) as share of state population.
items.method: CMS Medicare Enrollment Dashboard.

items.name: Marketplace enrollment
items.defn: Total ACA Marketplace plan enrollees (federal and state-based exchanges).
items.method: CMS Open Enrollment Period reports.

items.name: Employer-sponsored coverage
items.defn: Population reporting employer-sponsored insurance as their primary coverage.
items.method: American Community Survey 1-year estimates.

items.name: Avg marketplace premium
items.defn: Average monthly premium for the second-lowest-cost silver plan (the benchmark plan), 40-year-old non-smoker, before subsidies.
items.method: CMS Marketplace plan and rate filing data.
items.caveat: Pre-subsidy. Most enrollees pay significantly less after Advance Premium Tax Credits.

items.name: Medicaid expansion status
items.defn: Whether the state has adopted ACA Medicaid expansion (eligibility up to 138% of Federal Poverty Level for adults).
items.method: KFF tracker of state implementation status.

items.name: Certificate of Need scope
items.defn: Number of distinct healthcare service categories (e.g., new hospital beds, MRI machines, ASCs) that require state Certificate of Need approval.
items.method: NCSL and AHPA tracking of state CON laws.
items.caveat: Scope alone does not capture how restrictive enforcement is in practice.

items.name: NP scope of practice
items.defn: AANP classification: Full (NPs can practice independently), Reduced (must collaborate with physician), Restricted (must be supervised by physician).
items.method: American Association of Nurse Practitioners annual policy review.

items.name: Drug price legislation
items.defn: Number of state laws on drug pricing (transparency, price caps, PBM regulation, etc.) currently in effect.
items.method: NCSL state legislation database.

items.name: Telehealth parity laws
items.defn: Whether state requires private payers to cover and reimburse telehealth visits at parity with in-person visits.
items.method: Center for Connected Health Policy state policy tracker.

items.name: Rural health initiatives
items.defn: Number of distinct active state-level rural health programs (loan repayment, telehealth grants, rural hospital stabilization, etc.).
items.method: National Rural Health Association state policy compilation.

economics.source: Census ACS 2023 · BLS 2024 · USDA MTMG 2023 · MERIC 2024

items.name: Median household income
items.defn: Median annual household income in thousands of dollars, adjusted for inflation. A key driver of healthcare access — lower-income households delay care, skip prescriptions, and face higher effective cost burdens.
items.method: U.S. Census Bureau American Community Survey 1-year estimates.

items.name: Unemployment rate
items.defn: Percentage of the civilian labor force actively seeking but unable to find work. Job loss typically means loss of employer-sponsored insurance and income, directly increasing uninsured rates and deferred care.
items.method: Bureau of Labor Statistics Local Area Unemployment Statistics (LAUS) program.

items.name: Housing cost burden
items.sub: % households spending >30% of income on housing
items.defn: Share of households spending more than 30% of gross income on housing costs (rent or mortgage + utilities). High housing burden is a leading predictor of food insecurity, unmet medical needs, and mental health strain.
items.method: Census ACS estimate of cost-burdened households.

items.name: Food insecurity rate
items.defn: Percentage of households that lacked reliable access to enough food at some point during the year. Food insecurity is independently associated with worse diabetes management, higher hospitalization, and increased chronic disease burden.
items.method: USDA Map the Meal Gap county-level estimates aggregated to state.

items.name: Cost of living index
items.defn: Composite index of prices for groceries, housing, utilities, transportation, and healthcare relative to the national average. States above 100 are more expensive; high cost of living compresses real purchasing power even at similar income levels.
items.method: MERIC (Missouri Economic Research and Information Center) composite index.
items.caveat: Index methodology and basket weights vary slightly by release year.
